chief_complaint: A 57-year-old man presented with a 12-hour history of fever, chest tightness, and palpitations.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension and previous myocardial infarction. He presented to the emergency department with symptoms of fever, chest tightness, and palpitations.
exam: Vital signs: fever, normal blood pressure. Physical examination: chest tightness and palpitations.
labs: ECG: sinus rhythm with abnormal Q waves and ST-segment elevation (0.1-0.35 mV) in leads V1-V3. Positive COVID-19 polymerase chain reaction test. Beta-angle measurement and r'-wave triangle base duration consistent with Brugada phenocopy pattern.
